label: CRM real estate + WhatsApp
prompt: Build a CRM for a real estate agency. Agents manage leads, properties, and deals. Admin sees analytics. WhatsApp notifications when a deal closes.
expected_behavior: Full schema with role-gated data access, WhatsApp workflow stub triggered on deal status_changed, analytics endpoints admin-only, integration hook for whatsapp.send_template_message
known_challenges: Role-gated data filtering must appear in API schema not just auth
WhatsApp stub must trigger on deal.status == closed
Analytics endpoints must be admin-only across all layers

label: Task manager + Slack
prompt: Task manager for an engineering team. Tasks have due dates, assignees, priorities, and status. Team lead gets a Slack message when a task is overdue.
expected_behavior: Task entity with due_date, assignee, priority columns. Slack workflow stub triggered when task status_changed with condition due_date < now(). Integration hook for slack.send_message.
known_challenges: Overdue condition must appear in workflow stub trigger.condition
Slack integration hook must have channel and text as required_inputs
Team lead role must be defined in auth schema

label: Inventory + email alert
prompt: Inventory system for a warehouse. Products, stock movements, suppliers. Low stock triggers an email alert.
expected_behavior: Product entity with stock_level column. Gmail workflow stub triggered on stock_level updated with condition stock_level < threshold. Integration hook for gmail.send_email.
known_challenges: Low stock condition must appear in workflow stub
Email integration hook must reference gmail.send_email action
Stock movement as separate entity with product FK

label: HR tool + Slack
prompt: HR tool for a 50-person company. Track employees, leave requests, and performance reviews. Notify manager on Slack when leave is approved.
expected_behavior: LeaveRequest entity with status field. Slack workflow stub triggered on leave status_changed with condition status == approved. Manager role in auth schema.
known_challenges: Leave approval workflow must appear as workflow stub not just business rule
Manager notification must map leave.manager_id to Slack user_id
Performance review as separate entity

label: E-commerce + Stripe + Gmail
prompt: E-commerce backend. Products, orders, customers, payments via Stripe. Order confirmation sent via Gmail.
expected_behavior: Stripe workflow stub on User created, Gmail stub on Order created. Two integration hooks. Stripe customer_id stored on user table. tenant_id on all entities.
known_challenges: Two separate workflow stubs for two integrations
Stripe create_customer triggered on user registration
Gmail send_email triggered on order creation

label: Event management + WhatsApp
prompt: Event management platform. Organizers create events, attendees register, QR check-in at the door. Confirmation via WhatsApp.
expected_behavior: Event and Registration entities. WhatsApp stub triggered on Registration created. QR check-in as unauthenticated endpoint. Integration hook for whatsapp.send_template_message.
known_challenges: QR check-in must be unauthenticated in auth schema
WhatsApp stub must map attendee.phone to to field
Registration as junction entity between User and Event

label: Project tracker + Jira + Google Sheets
prompt: Project tracker. Projects, milestones, tasks. Sync tasks to Jira. Update a Google Sheet with weekly progress.
expected_behavior: Two workflow stubs: Jira on task created, Google Sheets on task updated. Both integrations stubbed (is_stub=true) but hooks and stubs must be present and valid.
known_challenges: Jira and google_sheets are stubbed integrations — hooks still valid
Two separate integration hooks with correct required_inputs
Milestone as parent entity of Task with FK

label: Minimal prompt
expected_behavior: HITL triggered immediately with targeted clarifying questions. System does not crash or hallucinate features. Pipeline completes or pauses gracefully.
known_challenges: Must not crash on 3-word prompt
HITL must fire with specific questions not generic ones
No features assumed without user confirmation

label: Notion for doctors
prompt: Build something like Notion for doctors.
expected_behavior: Ambiguous domain detected. System surfaces assumptions (medical notes, patient records, appointments?) or asks one clarifying question. Does not try to build generic Notion.
known_challenges: Domain is ambiguous - Notion-like but for medical use
Must surface at least 2 assumptions about medical features
HIPAA or data sensitivity should be noted in assumptions

label: Overscoped platform
prompt: A platform with login, payments, roles, real-time chat, file uploads, native mobile, analytics, and a marketplace.
expected_behavior: Scope flagged as extreme. System generates schema for realistic MVP subset and explicitly documents what was deferred. Does not attempt to build all features.
known_challenges: Must not generate schema for all 8 features
Deferred features must appear in assumptions
MVP scope decision must be documented

label: Conflicting domain
prompt: A CRM but also a project manager but also an invoicing tool.
expected_behavior: Domain conflict detected. System identifies incompatible primary entities (Contact vs Task vs Invoice). Documents decision to focus on one domain or asks user to choose.
known_challenges: Three conflicting entity domains
System must pick one or ask not guess all three
Conflict must appear in validation_report.conflicts

label: Vague modifier
prompt: Task manager, but make it smart.
expected_behavior: Vague modifier smart is undefined. System documents what smart means as an assumption (AI prioritization? auto-assignment? recommendations?) rather than inventing features.
known_challenges: smart is undefined and must not be silently ignored
At least one assumption must document the smart interpretation
Core task manager features must still be generated
